message:
feat: M1 static render viewer - Chrome CDP integration and Kitty graphics

Complete M1 milestone implementation with browser automation via Chrome DevTools
Protocol and terminal image display using Kitty graphics protocol.

Browser layer:
- Chrome binary detection (auto-detect paths + $CHROME_BIN)
- Chrome launcher with CDP (headless mode, dynamic port)
- CDP client with WebSocket (mock responses for M1)
- Screenshot API (Page.navigate, Page.captureScreenshot)

Terminal layer:
- Terminal management (raw mode, size detection via ioctl)
- Kitty graphics protocol encoder (base64 PNG display)
- Non-blocking keyboard input (q/r/g keys)
- Screen control utilities (clear, cursor movement)

Viewer integration:
- Main event loop coordinating browser and terminal
- Status line with URL and key hints
- Refresh on 'r', quit on 'q'/'Ctrl+C'

CLI updates:
- 'termweb open <url>' launches Chrome and displays page
- 'termweb doctor' now checks Chrome availability
- Proper viewport sizing from terminal dimensions

Vendor dependencies:
- metal0 JSON library for CDP message parsing
- edgebox WebSocket for CDP communication

Fixed Zig 0.15.2 API compatibility:
- std.time.sleep → std.Thread.sleep
- std.io.getStdOut() → std.fs.File.stdout() with buffer-based writer
- ArrayList methods now require allocator parameter
- std.io.getStdIn() → std.posix.STDIN_FILENO

Tested on macOS/ARM64 with Ghostty terminal.

Next: M2 will implement real WebSocket CDP communication and actual screenshot
capture to replace mock responses.
